title: UNDERCAST specimen
description: One performer record from data/specimens.json — a person who vanished under a designed face, mask, suit, mo-cap rig or an unseen voice. Field shape inferred from the 1067 live records and the fields index.html actually renders.

id.description: Catalog number. Used as the DOM data-uid, the permalink hash and the sort key. The optional 'G' marks a model-grown card (rendered with a Grown badge).

character.description: The designed character shown on the front of the card.

actor.description: The performer underneath, revealed on the back. Also the A–Z sort key.

aliases.description: Verified alternate professional names used only for identity matching and discovery.

production.description: The film / series / show the character appears in.

universe.description: The shelf the specimen files under. Drives the shelf filter chips.

years.description: Free-form run of years (e.g. "1993–99", "2001"). The first 4-digit year is bucketed into a decade chip.

designer.description: Free-form credit line for who built the face; parsed into browsable maker names.

transform.description: How far the performer vanished, 1 (barely) to 5 (unrecognisable). Rendered as five pips.

knownFor.description: A "you've also seen/heard them" note on the card back.

reveal.description: The payoff paragraph on the card back.

kind.description: Optional. "voice" swaps the relief art for a soundwave glyph and relabels the card. Absent means a face performer (the implicit default). NOTE: only "voice" occurs in the current data; "face" is the implied default index.html falls back to and is never written out.

still.description: Real character still shown on the card front, or null / absent to fall back to the explicit archival absence graphic.

portrait.description: Real actor portrait shown on the card back, or null / absent to fall back to the bare-face relief art.

conditions.description: Optional evidence-scoped physical or environmental performance conditions. Never infer these from an image; every entry requires a source URL.

references.description: Optional claim-level fact references. These support the performance, design, production and biographical text; image provenance remains in still/portrait and SOURCES.json.

performances.description: Additional exact performer-role credits anchored by this person record. These are evidence-bearing identities, not aliases inferred from the display title.

_grown.description: Optional. Marks a specimen added by the in-page replicator; renders a hatched front and a "Grown" badge. Not present in the current committed data.

_verified.description: Optional. Whether a grown specimen has been verified; controls the badge style. Not present in the current committed data.

roleAliases.description: Evidence-backed alternate labels for the primary displayed role (for example a source title without an in-universe rank). These are identity-equivalent names for the same role, not additional performances.

label.description: Human-readable description of what this source supports.

image.description: A still (front) or portrait (back) image reference. index.html only reads .src and .kind; .origin/.author/.license/.pin are provenance carried alongside.

src.description: Image path, relative to the site root (e.g. "images/uc-001-still.jpg"). Not a URL.

kind.description: Provenance class of a real sourced image. still.kind is always "still"; portrait.kind is "copyright" or "free". Missing evidence stays missing and is never replaced with a fabricated face.

origin.description: Source page the image was fetched from.

author.description: Optional credited author. May be an empty string when unknown/unneeded (e.g. copyright stills).

license.description: Optional licence label. May be an empty string when unknown/unneeded.

pin.description: Optional. Marks a curated/locked image choice. Only ever true in the current data.

focus.description: Optional curated focal point used when a source image must be cropped into a fixed comparison frame.

year.description: Optional. Year the photograph was taken, when known — provenance carried on 168 portraits. Not rendered; kept for citation/dating.
